_meta.calibration_source: ResumeAdapter Q1-2026 real pipeline data (overall median=48, pre-optimisation); CVCraft 2026 industry data (tech avg=65 post-optimisation); ResumeOptimizerPro 2026 (pre-optimisation avg=47 JD-match only); Maywise 2026 India lateral benchmark (70+ = passing threshold for most JD-specific checks)
notes: p50 anchors are calibrated to the composite score distribution of UNOPTIMISED real resumes at each seniority band.

IMPORTANT: prior version cited ResumeAdapter median=62, but that figure is post-optimisation average. Real first-submission median is 48/100 per ResumeAdapter Q1-2026. p50 anchors have been corrected downward for junior/mid/staff to reflect this.
Junior p50 corrected from 52 → 46: raw first-submission junior resumes cluster around the overall 48 median; no upward shift justified.
Mid p50 corrected from 58 → 54: mid-level resumes score modestly above overall median due to more experience content, but prior value of 58 was calibrated against optimised resumes.
Senior p50 unchanged at 63: this anchor accurately matches observed composite distribution for 7-12 YOE profiles and is corroborated across sources.
Staff p50 corrected from 66 → 62: staff/principal resumes are architecture-heavy and often fail keyword match, pulling composite down despite strong content. 62 reflects this.
p90 anchors unchanged: top ~10-12% of optimised resumes per ResumeOptimizerPro 2026 data (85+ = 11-15% of resumes). These are post-optimisation ceilings and remain valid.
Management bands (em, senior_em, director) added for completeness — percentile.py VALID_SENIORITY must be extended to include these or they will error.
EM p50 at 57: management-track resumes tend to score slightly below IC equivalents on ATS due to softer keyword density (less technical stack, more leadership language).
Senior EM p50 at 60, Director p50 at 62: slight upward progression as these profiles accumulate more formal keyword coverage.
Refresh cadence: review every 6 months. Re-anchor against corpus once n > 500 runs.
_meta.refresh_cadence: Review every 6 months. Re-anchor against corpus once n > 500 runs.
